subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the poem in depth, exploring complex imagery and symbolism
Examine the use of poetic devices such as personification, alliteration, repetition, and metaphor
Interpret philosophical and moral implications of the poem
Develop critical thinking by connecting nature’s power to human resilience and life lessons
Encourage reflective and analytical writing on abstract themes in poetry

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Context
topics_covered: Historical and cultural context of the poem
Introduction to themes: nature, power, resilience
teaching_strategy: Lecture on poet and literary context
student_activities: Take notes on the poet and poem context
Share thoughts on wind and nature’s power

lesson_plan.title: Detailed Analysis: Stanzas 1–2
topics_covered: Personification of the wind as a powerful force
Imagery of destruction and human vulnerability
Tone: warning and admiration for natural forces
teaching_strategy: Encourage visualization of imagery and symbols
student_activities: Write short interpretation of each stanza

lesson_plan.title: Stanzas 3–4: Human Response
topics_covered: Analysis of human preparation and resilience
Symbolism of strong homes, steadfast hearts, and disciplined bodies
teaching_strategy: Compare human resilience to natural challenges
student_activities: List examples of resilience in poem and real life

lesson_plan.title: Poetic Devices and Style
topics_covered: Effect of devices on meaning and mood
teaching_strategy: Analyze how they enhance the poem’s message

lesson_plan.title: Critical Reflection and Life Application
topics_covered: Abstract interpretation of poem’s philosophical meaning
Connecting nature’s challenges to human life
Discussion on personal development and resilience
teaching_strategy: Class debate on strength and preparation
Use real-life examples to link poem to experience
student_activities: Write reflection on human resilience and challenges

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Discussion, and Assessment
topics_covered: Recap of all stanzas and themes
Review of poetic devices and style
Discussion of abstract interpretations and life lessons
student_activities: Write a short analytical essay connecting poem to life lessons

teaching_aids: Charts showing literary devices and examples
Handouts with discussion and critical thinking questions
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze both literal and symbolic meanings of the poem, connecting poetic imagery to life lessons and human resilience.
